compiled_state.title: Implement ticket delete command (ticket-delete.sh + dispatch wiring)
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Context
Implements the ticket delete command. Tests are defined in T3 (9d89-9a01). The command must pass all 5 behavioral tests in T3 after this task is complete.

## What to do

### 1. Add dispatch case in `plugins/dso/scripts/ticket`
Add a `delete)` case to the `case $subcommand in` block (mirroring `archive)` at line 426):
```bash
delete)
    _ensure_initialized
    # shellcheck source=/dev/null
    source "$SCRIPT_DIR/ticket-lib-api.sh"
    _ticketlib_dispatch ticket_delete "$@"
    exit $?
    ;;
```

### 2. Add `ticket_delete` function in `plugins/dso/scripts/ticket-lib-api.sh`
Follow the `ticket_archive` function pattern (line 1587). Key steps inside the function:

#### --user-approved gate (DD1)
Parse `--user-approved` flag. If absent, print usage and exit 1.

#### Status gate for target ticket
Unlike `ticket_archive` (which only works on open tickets), `ticket_delete` MUST accept tickets in ANY non-deleted status (open, in_progress, closed). Do NOT inherit `ticket_archive`'s open-only gate. This allows deleting tickets that were closed or left in_progress.

#### Children guard (DD2)
Get children via `ticket deps <id>` (or scan LINK events in `$TRACKER_DIR` where `relation == "child"`). For each child: check if `.tombstone.json` OR `.archived` marker exists in the child's ticket dir. If any child lacks both markers (i.e., the child is NOT deleted), exit 1 with message: `"Cannot delete ticket '<id>': has non-deleted children: <child-ids>"`.

#### UNLINK scan
Iterate all ticket directories in `$TRACKER_DIR`. For each dir, scan `*-LINK.json` files. For each LINK event where `data.target_id == <deleted_id>` that is net-active (not cancelled by a corresponding UNLINK event), write an UNLINK event via `write_commit_event`.

#### ARCHIVED event (DD3)
Write an ARCHIVED event into the deleted ticket's dir via `write_commit_event`. This causes `ticket list` to exclude the ticket (reuses existing `exclude_archived=True` mechanism).

#### .tombstone.json (DD4)
Write `{"status": "deleted"}` as `.tombstone.json` into the deleted ticket's dir. Commit this file with: `git -C "$TRACKER_DIR" add "$TICKET_DIR/.tombstone.json" && git -C "$TRACKER_DIR" commit -q --no-verify -m "ticket: DELETE $ticket_id"`. This makes `_get_ticket_status()` return `"deleted"`, which combined with T2's _graph.py fix causes `ready_to_work` to clear for blocked tickets.

#### .archived marker
Call `write_marker "$TICKET_DIR"` (same as `ticket_archive` does post-commit).

## Files to modify
- `plugins/dso/scripts/ticket` — add `delete)` case at line ~432
- `plugins/dso/scripts/ticket-lib-api.sh` — add `ticket_delete` function after `ticket_archive`

## Acceptance criteria
- `ticket delete <id>` (no flag) exits 1 with usage message (DD1)
- `ticket delete <id> --user-approved` with non-deleted children exits 1 listing child IDs (DD2)
- `ticket delete <id> --user-approved` (no children, open or any status) exits 0; ticket absent from `ticket list` (DD3)
- After delete, `ticket deps <blocked-id>` has `ready_to_work: true` via tombstone chain (DD4)
- UNLINK events are written for all active LINK events pointing to the deleted ticket
- All 5 tests in T3 (9d89-9a01) pass after this task is complete

## Depends on
T3 (9d89-9a01) — RED tests must exist before GREEN implementation

## Testing mode
RED — implementing new ticket delete command to satisfy T3 RED tests

comments.body:
GAP_ANALYSIS AC_AMENDMENT (2 findings):

FINDING 1 - UNLINK scan must include deleted ticket's own outbound LINKs:
Add to T4 AC: the UNLINK scan must also scan the deleted ticket's own directory for outbound LINK events (where it is the source). For each such active outbound LINK, write an UNLINK event in the deleted ticket's directory AND (if the relation is reciprocal, e.g. relates_to) emit a reciprocal UNLINK in the partner's directory. This prevents surviving partners from observing stale active relations back to the deleted ticket.

FINDING 2 - Partial-failure ordering and idempotency:
Add to T4 AC: (a) Write UNLINK events FIRST (for all active inbound LINKs pointing to deleted ticket AND outbound LINKs from deleted ticket), THEN write ARCHIVED event, THEN .tombstone.json + .archived marker. (b) If re-invoked against an already-tombstoned ticket, skip gate/children-guard and complete any missing UNLINKs rather than erroring. This ensures partial-failure recovery converges correctly.
comments.author: Test
